articles.title: Is It Safe to Eat Fresh Produce? Here’s Everything to Know About the Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Across the U.S.
articles.content:
每周去超市采购时，我都会尽量多买新鲜水果和蔬菜。一盒浆果？放进购物车。袋装沙拉？简单的晚餐。新鲜香菜？必须的。但随着全国各地环孢子虫病疫情不断蔓延，我突然开始用不同的眼光看待农产品区了。

今年已有超过7000人在34个州感染了环孢子虫病——不幸的是，这个数字预计还会增加。虽然环孢子虫病在热带和亚热带地区更为常见，但它每年都会在美国引发疫情。事实上，病例往往在5月到8月间激增，这与感染相关的新鲜农产品最常出现在我们餐桌上有关。不过，这次疫情要严重得多。

好消息是？这不是彻底放弃你最爱超市的水果和蔬菜的理由。做一些聪明的替换——了解哪些食物目前风险较低——可以帮助你在享受农产品的同时保护自己。

继续阅读，了解为什么新鲜农产品是这次疫情的重要组成部分，哪些食物被认为是最安全的选择，以及在疫情平息之前你应该避免什么。

是时候上一堂简短（且易于理解）的科学课了。环孢子虫是一种微小的寄生虫，会导致环孢子虫病。当人们食用或饮用被含有寄生虫的粪便（是的，就是粪便）污染的食物或水时，这种疾病就会传播。新鲜水果和蔬菜可能在多个环节被污染，从种植的农场到收获和包装的设施。而且由于大多数都是生吃的，它们从未暴露在能杀死寄生虫的高温下。

环孢子虫的一个有趣特点是，它不会在人离开感染者身体后立即使人患病。它实际上需要在环境中停留数天到数周才能具有传染性。这意味着人们通常不会直接从病人那里感染——它更常与受污染的食物或水有关。

不幸的是，仅仅在厨房水龙头下冲洗你最喜欢的超市农产品并不总是足够的。这种寄生虫可以附着在娇嫩水果和绿叶蔬菜的表面，使其难以完全冲洗干净。

如果你想在这场疫情中更加谨慎行事，一般规则很简单：有保护性表皮、表面坚固或经过烹饪的食物是你的最佳选择。以下是一些最安全的选择：

如果可以的话，现在尽量避免以下食物。如果你决定吃它们，彻底烹饪后会安全得多。以下是你可能想要避免吃的食物：

你不必完全不吃农产品，但多加小心大有帮助。以下是一些降低环孢子虫病风险的食品安全提示。

环孢子虫不是你典型的24小时肠胃感冒。症状通常在吃了受污染食物约一周后出现，可能包括水样腹泻、胃痉挛、恶心、食欲不振、疲劳和体重减轻。如果不治疗，一些人会连续数周（甚至数月！）感到不适，症状反反复复。

如果你最近吃过新鲜农产品并出现持续性腹泻，尤其是持续超过两天，值得打电话给你的医疗服务提供者。幸运的是，环孢子虫病通常可以用处方抗生素治疗，早期诊断可以帮助你更快开始好转。
articles.summary:
On my weekly supermarket run, I always try to grab as many fresh fruits and veggies as possible. A container of berries? Into the cart. Bagged salad? Easy dinner. Fresh cilantro? Absolutely. But with a growing cyclospora outbreak making people sick across the country, I’m suddenly looking at the produce aisle a little differently.

More than 7,000 people have already gotten sick with cyclosporiasis across 34 states this year—and unfortunately, that number is expected to grow. And while cyclosporiasis is more common in tropical and subtropical parts of the world, it causes outbreaks in the U.S. every year. In fact, cases tend to spike between May and August, when fresh produce linked to infections is most commonly on our plates. That said, this outbreak is a lot worse.

The good news? This isn’t a reason to swear off your favorite grocery store’s fruits and vegetables altogether. A few smart swaps—and knowing which foods are a lower risk right now—can help you keep enjoying produce while protecting yourself.

Read on to learn why fresh produce is such a big part of this outbreak, which foods are considered the safest bets and what you should avoid until this outbreak settles down.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more news, health, food, fun facts, cleaning, travel and tech all week long.

Why is fresh produce such an issue with this cyclosporaiasis outbreak?

Time for a quick (and easy-to-understand) science lesson. Cyclospora is a tiny parasite that causes an illness called cyclosporiasis. It spreads when people eat food or drink water contaminated with feces (yes, poop) containing the parasite. Fresh fruits and vegetables can become contaminated at several points, from the farm where they’re grown to the facilities where they’re harvested and packaged. And since most are eaten raw, they’re never exposed to the high heat that would kill the parasite.

One interesting thing about cyclospora is that it doesn’t make people sick immediately after leaving an infected person’s body. It actually needs days to weeks in the environment to become infectious. That means people don’t typically catch it directly from someone who’s sick—it’s much more commonly linked to contaminated food or water.

And unfortunately, simply rinsing your favorite grocery store‘s produce under the faucet isn’t always enough. The parasite can cling to the surface of delicate fruits and leafy greens, making it difficult to wash away completely.

Which types of produce are safe to eat?

If you’re trying to play it safer while this outbreak is going on, the general rule is simple: Foods with protective skins, sturdy surfaces or that are cooked are your best bet. Here are some of the safest options:

  • Thick-peeled fruits: Bananas, oranges, pineapples, avocados and watermelons
  • Firm, smooth vegetables: Cucumbers, bell peppers, carrots and broccoli
  • Layered vegetables: Cabbage (discard the outer layers before washing)
  • Commercially frozen products: Frozen spinach, frozen berries and frozen vegetables
  • Cooked or processed foods: Canned items, baked goods and hot soups

Which types of produce should you skip?

If you can, avoid the below foods for now. If you do decide to eat them, they’re much safer when thoroughly cooked. Here’s what you might want to avoid eating:

  • Raw berries: Fresh raspberries, blackberries and strawberries
  • Soft-skinned fruits: Peaches, plums, nectarines and grapes
  • Raw leafy greens: Fresh spinach, romaine lettuce and kale
  • Prewashed salad mixes: Bagged salad kits and precut greens
  • Precut grocery fruit bowls: Mixed melon or berry cups
  • Fresh raw herbs: Cilantro, basil and parsley

What other safety tips should you keep in mind when eating fresh produce right now?

You don’t have to avoid produce completely, but a little extra caution goes a long way. Here are a few safety tips to reduce your risk of getting cyclosporiasis.

  • Wash all of your produce. Yes, even if you plan to peel it later. Washing won’t guarantee the parasite is removed, but it can help reduce dirt and other germs.
  • Cook when possible. High heat destroys cyclospora, making cooked vegetables much safer than raw ones.
  • Consider eating frozen produce. Commercially frozen fruits and vegetables are generally considered a lower-risk option because they’re processed under controlled conditions. Plus, they haven’t been commonly linked to outbreaks the way certain fresh produce items have.
  • Be careful when eating out. This includes all of the salads, garnishes and herbs served at restaurants. (That yummy fresh salsa is included too).
  • Stay on top of recalls. While there hasn’t been a specific brand tied to the outbreak, if one comes up, throw it away immediately. Don’t let the smell or look change your mind.

What happens if you get sick?

Cyclospora isn’t your typical 24-hour stomach bug. Symptoms often show up about a week after eating contaminated food and can include watery diarrhea, stomach cramps, nausea, loss of appetite, fatigue and weight loss. Without treatment, some people feel sick for weeks (or even months!) with symptoms that come and go.

If you’ve recently eaten fresh produce and develop persistent diarrhea, especially if it lasts more than a couple of days, it’s worth calling your health-care provider. Luckily, cyclospora can usually be treated with prescribed antibiotics, and getting diagnosed early can help you start feeling better sooner.

Sources:

  • Centers for Disease Control and Prevention: “Preventing Cyclosporiasis”
  • U.S. Food and Drug Administration: “Cyclosporiasis and Fresh Produce”
  • Centers for Disease Control and Prevention: “Food Poisoning Symptoms”

You Might Be Showering at the Wrong Time

Here's Why Mosquitoes Bite You More

Stop Making This Mistake with Your Allergy Meds

The post Is It Safe to Eat Fresh Produce? Here’s Everything to Know About the Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Across the U.S. appeared first on Reader's Digest.

articles.title: Which Harry Potter Character Are You, According to Your Zodiac Sign?
articles.content:
好吧，我确实把一个红发角色放进了最狂野、最急躁的火象星座。但你得承认罗恩确实有点火爆。他很容易被激怒，比如当赫敏取笑他的老鼠斑斑时。但你能指望什么呢？毕竟他的星座由火星——战神——掌管。你必须要有反应性，因为你生来就是为了战斗——尤其是为了保护你爱的人。当马尔福称赫敏为“泥巴种”时，罗恩是第一个拔出魔杖的人。即使腿受伤了，他也会挡在被认为是危险人物的小天狼星布莱克和哈利之间保护朋友。虽然火星掌管的星座能带来一股自信，但也意味着你可能有点过于争强好胜。白羊座是黄道十二宫的第一个星座，所以自然地，你总是想要争第一。但你内心深处觉得需要证明自己才能成为第一，就像罗恩一样。作为家里七个才华横溢的孩子之一，他必须为获得关注而竞争。白羊座常被称为“黄道十二宫的小宝宝”，因为像幼儿一样，这个星座的人得不到想要的东西时就会发脾气。回想一下赫敏和维克多·克鲁姆一起去圣诞舞会时，罗恩大发雷霆，因为他想带她去却没有勇气开口。

金牛座由金星——美与财富之星——掌管。我意识到海格并不让我们联想到美或财富，但金牛座也与大自然有着密切的联系——就像海格一样。这个星座与天蝎座（死亡）相对，与春天、生命和新的开始有关。这种与自然的固有联系也赋予了金牛座人对动物的特殊感情。说真的，你肯定在街上救过一只小猫。这个星座的某种特质就是知道如何吸引动物、理解它们并为它们发声。我现在能听到海格说：“巴克比克是一只好鹰头马身有翼兽——总是把自己的羽毛清理得干干净净。”

依赖原始的、自然的、有时简陋的家庭疗法，而不是任何医疗手段，也是非常金牛座的做法。例如，我的祖母（一个典型的金牛座）对姜茶、酊剂和粉末治疗一切疾病深信不疑。正是因为金牛座人与自然有着如此深厚的联系，他们确切地知道如何保持自己的健康和治愈。割伤用生龙肉？有谁能想到比那两个以机智著称的孪生子更好的双子座代表？作为一个双子座，你知道你被双子星座所象征，因为你有着双重性格。我确信你能在弗雷德和乔治身上看到自己的影子，他们有着聪明又孩子气的魅力，俏皮又爱玩的天性。很少有人能从双子座人的捉弄中逃脱。例子就是弗雷德和乔治经常取笑他们的哥哥珀西获得学生会主席这个庄重头衔。就像弗雷德和乔治一样，你也相当有创新精神和好奇心，这两个特质帮助这对兄弟创建了他们的生意——韦斯莱双子店。甚至这个生意本身——销售假装生病的产品、进行恶作剧或一般用来绕过规则——都与双子座调皮的天性密切相关。弗雷德和乔治也倾向于用谜语和双关语说话，这是一种经典的双子座特质，你可能很有共鸣。还记得他们对赫敏的回答吗？赫敏问他们从哪里弄到韦斯莱双子店的钱：“别问我们问题，我们就不会对你撒谎。”

作为家庭和归属的星座，巨蟹座最经典的特质是对所爱之人强烈的保护欲。多比保护哈利，没错，但他也在与那种自己不值得被爱的感觉作斗争。正是哈利对多比的接纳让他感到被爱，因此需要为他终身服务。作为一个巨蟹座，你可能能理解多比对哈利那种强烈、略带执着的爱——就像他给游走球施魔法去追杀哈利，或者藏起罗恩和赫敏写给哈利的所有信。这些行为近乎疯狂粉丝的行为，但你只是想让所爱的人安全！所以你爱得很用力，当你感到忠诚时会过度保护。当然，可能会有人被游走球击中脸，但巨蟹座也会给予最无私的关怀。多比冒着自身安全、承受马尔福家的惩罚，只是为了留在哈利身边并警告他即将到来的危险。这是我能想到的最无私的事情了。

人们经常认为哈利是狮子座——别误会我，狮子座可以很勇敢。（毕竟这是狮子座的象征！）但狮子座更阴暗的特质是吉德罗·洛哈特绝对拥有的那种：虚荣。诚实一点，你路过镜子或商店橱窗时多久看一次自己的倒影？是的，这里面有点虚荣。但嘿，你很美丽，你自己也知道！这就是虚荣和自信碰撞形成狮子座经典自我认同的地方——在哈利·波特系列中没有人比吉德罗·洛哈特更自恋。他对名声、美貌和公众关注的吸引力，他的首要任务是保持美丽和被仰慕。有趣的是，这也是你作为狮子座的主要使命。你喜欢站在聚光灯下，不用刻意就能吸引注意力，而且是一个非常熟练的故事讲述者。洛哈特确实有讲故事的能力，他所有的书都是证明。他能够如此详细地讲述故事，以至于没有人意识到他从未做过其中任何一件。狮子座有趣的地方在于这个星座感觉很真实，即使狮子座人害怕完全做自己会被拒绝（对吧？）洛哈特展示了这一点。

啊，处女座：正义、精确、追求完美、脚踏实地的。你可能因为赫敏的万事通性格（尤其是在前几部电影中）而与她产生共鸣，但麦格教授有着更接地气的土象能量。像你一样，她不太愿意冒险，在不确定时总是遵守规则。（赫敏，我们知道，并不是这样。）作为一个处女座，你由水星——沟通之星——掌管，所以你与文字有着深厚的联系，并善于利用这一点。麦格教授经常被忽视的一点是她极其敏锐的智慧和冷面幽默。无论是开玩笑说她会把罗恩或哈利变成一只怀表，这样他们中的一个就能准时，还是告诉哈利如果他死了就不需要交作业，她的讽刺名言都是传奇。但我认为麦格教授最符合处女座特质的特质是她的谦逊和勤奋——我确信你很理解这一点。她不知疲倦地在幕后工作，给学生提供成功所需的一切，却不要求任何荣誉。这一点在她在哈利一年级时推荐他加入魁地奇球队甚至给他买了第一把扫帚时就能看出来。

没有什么比友好、公正、对所有人和动物都人道更能代表天秤座了。哈利·波特（还有你）就是这样的人。以他对弱者的同情心著称——比如巴克比克、多比和纳威·隆巴顿——哈利一次又一次地为那些无法为自己站起来的人挺身而出。例如，当学校大多数人在取笑卢娜·洛夫古德时，哈利总是邀请她坐在自己身边，让她感到被接纳。你是天秤座的象征，这意味着你非常善于外交。天秤座总是在寻求和谐，我确信你在各种情况下都是调解者——就像哈利经常发现自己处于罗恩和赫敏之间或者马尔福和他欺负的人之间的争吵中间。但天秤座还有一种独特的能力：能够看透别人的眼睛。这就是帮助你如此深刻地连接他人的原因。这种富有同理心的视角让天秤座人成为调解者。而且，当然，它是哈利的一个主要可辨识特质，即使面对最黑暗的敌人——比如德拉科·马尔福和伏地魔——他也能原谅他们，即使这对自己不利。

天蝎座由冥王星——冥界之星——掌管，作为一个逃离死亡又复活的人，神秘人正是冥界运作的 literal 体现。你知道你有点喜欢伏地魔的黑暗面！他毫无顾忌地揭露人们最深的秘密，因为他不怕黑暗——他就生活在其中。让我们也谈谈那些绰号，“神秘人”和“那个不能被提名字的人”。伏地魔通过一件事建立了他的权力：神秘感。他拥有的力量在于没有人类形态——其他人都不知道他在哪里、他变得多么强大或者他想要什么。老实说，当你被逼到墙角时，这是一个你可能也会用的典型天蝎座策略：不给任何可被利用的个人信息。天蝎座也是与死亡最相关的星座。J·K·罗琳说过伏地魔的灵感来自法语词汇，意为“飞行”或“死亡之偷”。通过将自己的灵魂分裂成不同的魂器，伏地魔本质上是在欺骗死亡、骗取永生——这是只有天蝎座才会梦想做的事情。另外，伏地魔是阴森的，作为一个天蝎座，你喜欢万圣节、鬼魂和黑暗。你以阴森为荣。看到了吗？很契合。

这一个对某些人来说可能有点难卖，但听我说完。就像我之前说的，赫敏绝对是个万事通。但在她学术和好好先生的外表下，她实际上打破的规则比遵守的还多——而且似乎总是因此惹上麻烦。想想法她非法酿制复方汤剂、闯入图书馆的禁书区或者滥用时间转换器去上更多课程。我打赌在你内心深处，你为她的胆大妄为鼓掌，因为你自己也违反过一些规则。但这个冒险的火象星座对规则的倾向也让她——还有你——成为领导者。通常是赫敏迈出探索未知的第一步，而不是哈利或罗恩。毕竟是她最先发起邓布利多军的！别被你那狂野的一面所迷惑。虽然你是个打破规则的人，但像赫敏一样，你也非常有智慧。人马座以高层次的学术、哲学和大局思维著称。

如果你对多洛雷斯·乌姆里奇有什么印象的话，那就是她墙上贴满的“教育法令”。换句话说，规则。让我们在这里明确一件事。她不是喜欢遵守规则；她喜欢制定规则。我能想到的更符合摩羯座特质的，莫过于夺取控制权、在任何人有机会质疑之前就定下法律。就像乌姆里奇一样，你可能也有对阶级和风度的品味。你甚至可能像她一样穿着粗花呢裙子和衬衫。但摩羯座还有其他的：一种让你想要成为老板的支配性人格。乌姆里奇实际上是自己任命了一个领导职位——只有摩羯座才有这种魄力。当然，她可能有点权力饥渴和控制欲（也是典型的摩羯座），但她管理得井井有条。除非你按她的方式做，否则什么都不会完成，对吧？正是对领导力、地位权力的追求让她如此摩羯座。

邓布利多是波特系列中一个非常睿智和强大的形象。作为一个天生的领导者，他在一切事情上采取公正的行动，这也是你非常擅长的。水瓶座是社群的星座，所以让每个人团结在一起对你很重要——类似于邓布利多对保持学校及其学生联系的热情。你可能分享的一个非常水瓶座的邓布利多特质（虽然有点不体面）是他在情感上与一个人或情况脱离的能力。他可以像开关一样打开或关闭自己的感情，就像他把年幼的哈利扔进三强争霸赛，或者利用他来接近伏地魔一样。

这看似冷酷，但水瓶座的核心是以客观视角审视一切。如邓布利多，你做正确之事时分析且战略性强——近乎无情。许多水瓶座因这种逻辑思维被视为无情。邓布利多确有争议教学法，例如纵容斯内普欺凌学生，但这正合水瓶座特质。不惹争议便非水瓶座。卢娜与纳吉：外表看卢娜似疯子，双鱼座能量亦然。（抱歉，双鱼座，但事实如此。）双鱼掌管物理世界不可见之物——包括梦想、魔法、直觉、潜意识、想象力、阈限空间及“彼世”。你可能如卢娜般超然神秘，仿佛见所未见世界。她确实见纳吉、瓦克普特与夜骐（唯见死亡者可见之骨翼马）。当梦境映照现实，你不能不称奇。双鱼最连鬼魂灵体，或有过通灵经历。卢娜虽受欺凌仍做自己，此点惹人爱，因你亦常感被误解。作为双鱼，你对世界（此世与彼世）的广阔视野使你疏离社会以求舒适。《读者文摘》提供易懂得占星指南，从理解太阳星座、月亮星座和上升星座，到探索各星座优势、挑战与欲望。你将学习爱情、事业、爱好等理想匹配，及大型占星事件的影响。我们致力于高质量内容，由具有专业经验的作家撰写并咨询合格专家。依赖可靠来源如执业占星师及作者适当经历。核实所有事实数据，用可信来源支持并定期回顾以确保准确及时。阅读更多关于团队、贡献者与编辑政策。帖子《根据星座，你是哪个哈利·波特角色？》首发于《读者文摘》。
articles.summary:
Aries: Ron Weasley

(March 21–April 19)

OK, I did put a redhead in the most feral and impatient fire sign of them all. But you have to admit that Ron is a bit of a hothead. He’s so easily flustered, like when Hermione picks on his rat, Scabbers. But what do you expect when your sign is ruled by Mars, the planet of anger and war? You have to be reactive, because you were born to fight—especially to protect the ones you love. Ron was the first to draw a wand when Malfoy called Hermione a “filthy mudblood.” And even with an injured leg, he steps in between the assumed-dangerous Sirius Black and Harry to protect his friend.

Though being Mars-ruled can provide a burst of confidence, it also means you can be a little overly competitive. Aries is the first sign of the zodiac, so naturally, you always want to be first. But something in you feels like you need to prove yourself in order to be first, just like Ron. As one of seven talented children in the family, he had to compete for attention.

Aries is often referred to as “the baby” of the zodiac because, like a toddler, people born under this sign are known to throw tantrums when they don’t get their way. Just think back to when Hermione goes to the Yule Ball with Victor Krum. Ron threw a hissy fit because he wanted to take her but wasn’t brave enough to ask.

Taurus: Hagrid

(April 20–May 20)

Taurus is ruled by Venus, the planet of beauty and money. Hagrid doesn’t remind us of beauty or money, I realize, but Taurus is also highly connected to nature—just like Hagrid. This is the sign opposite Scorpio (death), and it’s associated with springtime, life and new beginnings.

This inherent bond to nature also gives Taurus natives a special connection to animals. Like, let’s be real: You’ve definitely saved a baby kitten off the street. Something about this sign just knows how to draw animals in, understand them and be an advocate. I can hear Hagrid now, saying, “Buckbeak was a good hippogriff—always cleaned his feathers.”

Relying on raw, natural and sometimes shabby home remedies, as opposed to anything medical, is also a very Taurus thing to do. For example, my grandmother (a stereotypical Taurus) swears by ginger teas, tinctures and powder for everything. And it’s because Taureans have such a deep connection with nature that they know exactly how to keep themselves healthy and healed. Raw dragon meat for cuts, anyone?

Gemini: Fred and George Weasley

(May 21–June 20)

Can you imagine a better Gemini representation than the infamous wit-centric twins themselves? As a Gemini, you know you’re symbolized by the celestial Twins because of your dual nature. And I’m sure you can see yourself in Fred and George, with their intelligent, childlike charm and cheeky, playful nature. Very few can get by a Gemini without being teased. Case in point: Fred and George often make fun of their brother, Percy, for holding the stately title of Head Boy.

Like Fred and George, you’re also quite innovative and curious, two traits that helped the brothers when creating their business, Weasley & Weasley. Even the business itself—selling products to fake sickness, perform practical jokes or generally pass by the rules—is intimately aligned with Gemini’s mischievous nature.

Fred and George also tend to speak in riddles with plays on words, a classic Gemini-ism that you can likely relate to. Remember their answer to Hermione, who asked where they got the money for Weasley & Weasley: “Ask us no questions and we’ll tell you no lies.”

Cancer: Dobby

(June 21–July 22)

The sign of home and family, Cancer’s most classic trait is a fierce protectiveness toward loved ones. Dobby protects Harry, yes, but he also fights the consistent feeling that he is unlovable. It’s Harry’s acceptance of Dobby that makes him feel loved, and therefore in need of his lifelong service.

As a Cancer, you can probably relate to Dobby’s intense, slightly obsessive love for Harry—like when he enchanted a bludger to go after Harry or hid all his letters from Ron and Hermione. These kinds of actions border those of a deranged fan, but you just want your loved ones to be safe! So you love hard, coddling when you feel devoted.

Sure, someone might get a bludger to the face, but Cancers also give the most selfless kind of care. Dobby risks his own safety and subjects himself to punishment from the Malfoys, just to stay close to Harry and warn him of approaching danger. That’s the most selfless thing I can think of.

Leo: Gilderoy Lockhart

(July 23–Aug. 22)

People often think of Harry as a Leo—and don’t get me wrong, Leos can be brave. (This is the sign of the Lion, after all!) But Leo’s more shadowy trait is one that Gilderoy Lockhart definitely possesses: vanity. Be honest, how often do you look at your reflection when passing by a mirror or store window? Yeah, there’s a little vanity in that. But hey, you’re beautiful and you know it!

This is where vanity and assuredness collide to become Leo’s classic self-confidence—and no one in the Harry Potter series screams narcissism like Gilderoy Lockhart. With a draw to fame, good looks and the public eye, his top priority is looking beautiful and being admired. And funny enough, this is your main mission as well. As a Leo, you adore being in the spotlight, can hold attention without trying and are a very skilled storyteller.

Lockhart definitely has the storytelling skill, with all his books. He’s able to share tales with such detail that no one even realizes he’s never done any of it. The funny thing about Leo is that this sign feels very authentic, even when Leos are afraid to be completely themselves for fear of being rejected (right?). Lockhart shows that story.

Virgo: Professor McGonagall

(Aug. 23–Sept. 22)

Ah, Virgo: righteous, precise, perfectionistic and grounded. You might relate to Hermione because of her know-it-all nature (especially in the first few movies), but Professor McGonagall has more grounded earth energy. Like you, she’s less of a risk-taker and always sticks to the rules when she’s unsure. (Hermione, we know, does not.)

As a Virgo, you’re ruled by Mercury, the planet of communication, so you have a deep connection with words and use that to your advantage. What often goes unmentioned about McGonagall is her incredibly sharp wit and deadpan humor. Whether it’s joking that she’ll turn Ron or Harry into a pocket watch so one of them will be on time, or telling Harry that if he dies he doesn’t need to turn in his homework, her sardonic one-liners are legendary.

But the most Virgo-esque trait I can think of in Professor McGonagall is her humbleness and hard work—something I’m sure you understand well. She tirelessly works behind the scenes to give the students everything they need to succeed, without asking for any of the credit. That much can be seen when she recommends Harry to the Quidditch team in his first year and even buys him his first broom.

Libra: Harry Potter

(Sept. 23–Oct. 22)

Nothing says Libra like being friendly, fair and humane to all people and animals. Harry Potter (and you) are just that. Known for his compassion for underdogs—like Buckbeak, Dobby and Neville Longbottom—Harry is seen time and time again standing up for those who can’t stand up for themselves. For example, when most of the school makes fun of Luna Lovegood, Harry consistently invites her to sit with him so she feels included.

You’re the sign of the Scales, which means you’re extremely diplomatic. A Libra is always seeking harmony, and I’m sure you’re the pacifier in all sorts of situations—just like Harry, who often finds himself in the middle of squabbles between Ron and Hermione or Malfoy and whoever he’s picking on.

But with Libra, there’s also this unique ability to see into the eyes of other people. That’s what helps you connect so deeply. This empathetic perspective is what allows Librans to become pacifiers. And, of course, it’s a major identifiable trait in Harry, as he forgives his darkest enemies—like Draco Malfoy and Voldemort—even to his detriment.

Scorpio: Voldemort

(Oct. 23–Nov. 22)

Scorpio is ruled by Pluto, the planet of the Underworld, and as someone who escapes mortality and comes back to life, You-Know-Who is a literal representation of the Underworld at work. And you know you kind of love the dark side of Voldemort! He shamelessly reveals people’s deepest secrets because he’s not afraid of the darkness—he lives it.

Let’s also talk about those nicknames, You-Know-Who and He-Who-Must-Not-Be-Named. Voldemort has created his power through one thing: mystery. The power he holds is in not having a human form—others are clueless about where he is, how powerful he’s become or what he wants. To be honest, this is a telltale Scorpio tactic you probably use when you’re backed into a corner: Give zero personal information that can be used against you.

Scorpio is also the sign most associated with death. J.K. Rowling has said that Voldemort was inspired by French words meaning “flight” or “theft” of death. By splitting his soul into different horcruxes, Voldemort essentially cheats death and tricks his way into immortality—something only a Scorpio would dream of doing. Plus, Voldemort is spooky, and as a Scorpio, you love Halloween, ghosts and darkness. You take pride in being spooky. See? It fits.

Sagittarius: Hermione Granger

(Nov. 22–Dec. 21)

This one might be a tough sell for some folks, but hear me out. Like I said earlier, Hermione is definitely a know-it-all. But underneath her academic and do-gooder personality, she actually breaks more rules than she follows—and it always seems to get her into trouble.

Just think about the time she illegally brewed polyjuice potion, broke into the restricted section of the library or abused the use of a time-turner to attend more classes. I bet in the back of your mind, you cheered her on for being so brazen because you’ve broken a few rules yourself. But this adventurous fire sign’s propensity to bend the rules also allows her—and you—to be a leader. It’s often Hermione who takes the first step into the unknown, not Harry or Ron. She’s the one who first initiated Dumbledore’s army, after all!

Don’t be fooled by your wild-child side, though. You may be a rule-breaker, but like Hermione, you’re also incredibly intellectual. Sagittarius is known for high-minded academics, philosophy and big-picture thinking.

Capricorn: Dolores Umbridge

(Dec. 22–Jan. 19)

If there’s one thing you might remember about Dolores Umbridge, it’s her wall-full of “educational decrees.” In other words, rules. And let’s be clear on one thing here. It’s not that she likes following rules; she likes setting them. I can’t think of a more Capricorn trait than seizing control and laying down the law before anyone has a chance to question it.

Like Umbridge, you may also have a taste for class and poise. You might even dress in tweed skirts and blouses like her. But Capricorns also have something else: a domineering personality that makes them want to be the boss. Umbridge practically appointed herself to a position of leadership—something only a Capricorn would have the gusto to do.

Sure, she may be a little power-hungry and controlling (also very typical Cap), but she runs a tight ship. And nothing will get done unless you do it the way you want, right? It’s the call to leadership, status and power that makes her such a Capricorn.

Aquarius: Albus Dumbledore

(Jan. 20–Feb. 18)

Dumbledore is an incredibly wise and powerful figure in the Potter series. A natural leader, he takes fair action in everything he does, which is something you’re also very good at. Aquarius is the sign of community, so keeping everyone together is important to you—similar to Dumbledore’s passion for keeping the school and its students connected.

One very Aquarian Dumbledore trait that you might share (and one that’s a bit unseemly) is his ability to emotionally detach from a person or situation. He can turn his feelings on or off, like when he tosses Harry into the Tri-Wizard Tournament as a young boy or uses him to get to Voldemort. This can feel downright cold-blooded, but at the heart of it all, being an Aquarius means you see all sides with an objective mind.

Like Dumbledore, you’re analytic and strategic—but borderline ruthless—when it comes to doing the right thing. For many Aquarians, this kind of logical mindset leads people to consider them heartless or merciless. And Dumbledore does have some questionable methods of teaching—for example, allowing Severus Snape to bully his students. But this makes him a perfect Aquarian candidate. If you’re not ruffling feathers by doing things differently, you’re not an Aquarius.

Pisces: Luna Lovegood

(Feb. 19–March 20)

Ah, Luna and her Nargles. From the outside, Luna looks like a nutcase. And to be honest, Pisces energy has that vibe. (Sorry, Pisces, but you know it’s true.) This is the sign that rules the things we can’t see in the physical world—including dreams, magic, intuition, the subconscious, imagination, liminal space and “the other side.” So, you might act a bit like Luna: detached and mystified, as if seeing a world beyond the one we live in.

And the thing is, she does! She sees Nargles, Wrackspurts and Thestrals (you know, the skeletal and winged horses only people who have seen death can see). You can’t say that you’re not mystified when your dreams actually mirror the real world, right? But it makes total sense because Pisces is the sign most connected to ghosts and spiritual beings. You may have even had psychic experiences!

Despite being picked on by the school bullies, Luna remains exactly who she is—which is something you love about her. And that’s because you probably feel misunderstood sometimes too. As a Pisces, your wide views of the world (this one and beyond) can make you feel a little different, so you tend to stay on the outskirts of society because it’s more comfortable.

Why trust us

Reader’s Digest provides an easy-to-understand guide to all things astrology—from understanding sun signs, moon signs and rising signs to exploring each zodiac sign’s strengths, challenges and desires. You’ll learn about your ideal match in love, career, hobbies and more, as well as learn how big astrological events will affect you. We’re committed to producing high-quality content by writers with expertise and experience in their field in consultation with relevant, qualified experts. We rely on reputable primary sources, including practicing astrologers, as well as our writers’ personal experiences where appropriate. We verify all facts and data, back them with credible sourcing and revisit them over time to ensure they remain accurate and up to date. Read more about our team, our contributors and our editorial policies.

The post Which <i>Harry Potter</i> Character Are You, According to Your Zodiac Sign? appeared first on Reader's Digest.
